Name: Hyprstream Monitors
Author: danmaxis
Description: Calm, glanceable system displays for Stream Deck on Linux. A parametric System Vitals key (CPU, RAM, battery or temperature — pick one per key) and a Clock & Timers key (wall clock, system uptime, OBS stream time or OBS session), plus a threshold-alerting key and a live OBS encoder-health key. Read straight from /proc, /sys and obs-websocket.

Actions.Name: System Vitals
Actions.Tooltip: One key, one system value — pick CPU usage, RAM usage, battery, or temperature from the dropdown. Add several keys, each set to a different variable. Read from /proc and /sys with color-coded warn/crit thresholds (and a charging-bolt for battery). Display-only. Works on any Linux setup (no Hyprland required).

Actions.Name: Clock & Timers
Actions.Tooltip: One key, one time value — pick wall clock, system uptime, OBS stream time, or OBS session from the dropdown. Clock supports 24h/12h with optional seconds and date; uptime reads /proc/uptime; stream time reads the live OBS output duration over obs-websocket. (OBS session = time since this plugin connected to OBS, not the OBS process start.) The OBS modes open a connection only when selected. Requires OBS 28+ with obs-websocket v5 for the OBS timers.

Actions.Name: Threshold Alert
Actions.Tooltip: An alerting key, not just a readout: pick a metric (CPU, RAM, temperature, battery) and a threshold, and the key stays calm until the value breaches it — then it escalates to a blinking red ALERT state so a problem catches your eye at a glance. 'Above' fires when the value climbs past the threshold (CPU/temp); 'Below' fires when it drops under it (battery). Reads /proc and /sys, no Hyprland required.

Actions.Name: OBS Stream Health
Actions.Tooltip: Live OBS encoder/output health on a key, color-coded green/amber/red — dropped frames, encoding lag, OBS CPU, or active FPS, read over obs-websocket v5. This is exactly what Linux software-encoder (x264/VAAPI) streamers lack: a glanceable warning before a stream degrades. The key's OBS dot turns green when connected. Enable the server in OBS: Tools → WebSocket Server Settings. Requires OBS 28+ with obs-websocket v5.

Actions.Name: Threshold Overlay
Actions.Tooltip: React to system or OBS metrics by driving on-stream overlays: when a metric (CPU, RAM, temperature, battery, or OBS dropped-frames/encode-lag/OBS-CPU/FPS) crosses a threshold — above or below — set an OBS text source, show/hide a source, and/or swap an image file. One key holds a list of rules; each fires on the transition and restores when the value clears (with a hysteresis dead-band to stop flapping). Overlays are restored when the key is removed. Requires OBS 28+ with obs-websocket v5.
